summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering police department recognition for an aggravated murder case arrest, retirement of K-9 unit 6, approval of tactical shield purchases for officer safety, termination of the tornado state of emergency from July 2022, adoption of revised cemetery fees, renewal of healthcare benefits with a 0% increase, and approval of a new FOP labor agreement. The board also discussed a temporary dwelling zoning text amendment and approved various administrative travel and appointments.
decisions: Motion to amend agenda to add item H1 - passed
Motion to adopt amended agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Nov 8-28, 2022) - passed
Motion to accept ballistic vest donation from Blue Line Canine Association for K-9 Kircher - passed
Motion to purchase tactical shield and case from Baker Ballistics not to exceed $7,200 - passed
Motion to present temporary dwelling unit text amendment (Section 7.02) to zoning commission with expanded verbiage for acts of nature - passed
Motion to declare surplus and obsolete eight microphones - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Administrator Rose to attend OTA conference ($215 registration) - passed
Motion to approve $125 registration fee for each trustee to attend OTA Winter Conference - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for HR Manager Young to attend SERB Academy training ($325 registration) - passed
Motion to adopt resolution declaring termination of state of emergency from July 6, 2022 storm damage - passed
Motion to adopt revised cemetery fee schedule effective December 1, 2022 - passed
Motion to renew healthcare plan with Medical Mutual for 2023 with 0% increase - passed
Motion to adjourn to executive session per ORC 121.22(G)(4) - passed
Motion to approve Brandon Oki as alternate member of zoning commission for two-year term - passed
Motion to approve FOP labor agreement (2022-MED-090791) for Jan 1, 2023 through Dec 31, 2025 - passed
Motion to approve elected officials and non-union wages schedule effective Jan 1, 2023 - passed
Motion to adjourn at 9:40 PM - passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to prepare motion for next meeting to purchase two additional tactical shields ($14,400) using ARP funds
Director Holbrook to modify temporary dwelling text amendment to include acts of nature (fire, tornado, etc.) and present to zoning commission
Chad to distribute revised cemetery fee schedule to all funeral homes effective December 1, 2022
HR Manager Young to continue working with broker on dental insurance alternatives
Get a picture of K-9 6 to put on the wall
Mikayla to monitor condition of cemetery chapel for insurance and safety purposes
Captain Hampton to get retirement plaque photo with Officer Bucksat and K-9 6
key_amounts: $7,200 - tactical shield purchase from Baker Ballistics
$14,400 - proposed purchase of two additional tactical shields at next meeting using ARP funds
$2,500 - estimated cost of donated ballistic vest for K-9 Kircher
$215 - registration cost for Administrator Rose OTA conference
$125 - registration fee per trustee for OTA Winter Conference
$325 - registration cost for HR Manager Young SERB Academy training
0% increase on 2023 Medical Mutual healthcare premiums
$5,000 HSA contribution for married, $1,000 for employee+kids, $500 for single
$750 proposed resident cemetery plot fee (up from $500)
$1,200 Hamilton Township resident burial comparison rate
8% proposed dental rate increase from Superior Dental (under review)
notable_topics: Recognition of police department for November 10 aggravated murder/shooting arrest
K-9 Unit 6 retirement ceremony after spinal injury
Ballistic vest donation for K-9 Kircher from Blue Line Canine Association
Tactical shield purchase for officer safety due to uptick in violent crime
Zoning text amendment for temporary dwelling units during home construction and acts of nature
Termination of state of emergency from July 6, 2022 tornado
Revised cemetery fee schedule to address fund shortfall
2023 healthcare benefits renewal with 0% premium increase
Elected officials and non-union wage schedule for 2023
Service department snow plow painting program with local high school
Station 18 renovation update with architect meeting
Clermont County Township Association meeting topics including indigent burials
notes: Trustee Allen was absent. Trustee Hauserman was elected president of the Clermont County Township Association. The meeting included an executive session from approximately 8:01 PM to 9:37 PM to discuss employment and compensation of public employees. The cemetery fee schedule had not been substantially updated since approximately 1998-1999, and the cemetery fund was described as 'going broke' and 'in the red.' The video title says 11.29.22 but the transcript opening references '9, 2022 at 7 p.m.' which appears to be November 29, 2022 with the date partially cut off. The painted snow plows story was featured on WLWT news. The November 10 shooting incident involved 17 rounds hitting one home, two entering an occupied bedroom, and six vehicles struck.
title: BOT 11.29.22.mp4
